question_title: Will the IOM Missing Migrants Project record at least 1,000 Mediterranean migrant deaths in 2026 before May 2026?
summary: The IOM Missing Migrants Project recorded 606 dead+missing in the Mediterranean by January 26, 2026 - the deadliest start to a year since tracking began in 2014. Central Mediterranean deaths alone reached 501 by February 21. The current total (Feb 26) is estimated at 700-900. Reaching 1,000 by end of April requires only ~100-300 more deaths over 65 remaining days, which translates to a required rate of just 2-5/day - well below the current pace of ~10+/day and even below the 2024 annual average of ~6.3/day. The threshold is very likely to be reached unless an unprecedented near-complete halt in crossings occurs.

factors.description: 606 dead+missing already recorded by Jan 26 (day 26), described by IOM as deadliest start to a year since 2014. Verified by multiple independent news sources.
501 Central Med deaths alone through Feb 21 (~9.6/day); total Mediterranean including Eastern and Western routes is higher. Current total estimated at 700-900.
Only ~200-350 more deaths needed to reach 1,000 in 65 remaining days. Required rate of 3-5/day is well below current pace and below recent annual averages.
No evidence of major policy changes, naval blockades, or other factors that would drastically reduce crossing attempts in the next 2 months
IOM data reporting may lag - some late April incidents might not appear in the dataset by May 1 resolution date
Small residual probability of unprecedented dramatic drop in crossings due to unknown policy or weather factors

reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.93 (93% YES)**

Key reasoning:
- **Already ~70% of the way there**: 606 Mediterranean deaths were recorded by January 26, and ~501 Central Med deaths by February 21. The current total is likely 700-900.
- **Low bar remaining**: Only ~200-350 more deaths needed over 65 days (through end of April). This requires just 3-5 deaths/day — well below the current pace of ~10/day and below even the 2024 annual average of ~6.3/day.
- **Record-setting pace**: IOM called 2026 the "deadliest start to a year in the Mediterranean since IOM began recording such data in 2014."
- **Main downside risks**: IOM reporting lag near the deadline, or an unprecedented near-complete halt in crossings. Both are low probability given the structural factors driving migration.

resolution_criteria: This question resolves to Yes if the IOM Missing Migrants Project incident dataset shows that the year-to-date sum of dead plus missing through the end of April 2026 for “Mediterranean” is at least 1,000.The primary source is the IOM Missing Migrants Project public site : https://missingmigrants.iom.int/region/mediterranean
background_info:
This tracks the scale of lethal migration risk in the Mediterranean in early 2026, which is relevant for humanitarian planning and policy response.
